overview: This Legacy mono-white archetype leverages Yorion, Sky Nomad to generate exponential Spirit token armies, then locks them in with Solitude for indestructibility and size. Stoneforge Mystic provides artifact synergy to accelerate the board state and close games. The deck operates as a combo-focused tempo strategy, aiming to establish an unblockable, unkillable token presence before opponents can stabilize.
win_conditions: Generate a massive Spirit token army using Yorion, Sky Nomad's triggered ability
Grant indestructible and +1/+1 to all Spirits via Solitude to overwhelm opponents in combat
Fetch and play key artifacts with Stoneforge Mystic to enable token generation or finish games

keep_criteria: Hands containing two or more lands alongside Yorion, Sky Nomad or Solitude
Hands with Stoneforge Mystic and a cheap artifact or land drop
Hands that can establish early board presence or protect key combo pieces
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, a turn-1 play (Thalia's Lieutenant, early removal, or Solitude), and a way to cast your 4-drops.
Keep Stoneforge Mystic if you have a land and can cast it for 2 mana.
Keep Yorion if you have 3+ lands or a way to accelerate it.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking lands or key signature cards
Hands with only high-cost spells and no early interaction
Hands that cannot efficiently cast Yorion, Sky Nomad or Solitude by turn three
Mulligan pure land, pure removal, or hands with no early interaction/land base.

early_game: Prioritize land drops to ensure mana availability for Stoneforge Mystic and early spells
Cast Stoneforge Mystic to search for artifacts that support token generation or board control
Play cheap spells or hold mana to protect against early disruption
Turn 1: Play land, cast Thalia's Lieutenant or a removal spell if possible.
If opening Stoneforge Mystic, play land, cast Mystic, tap for mana, search for Batterskull or a threat, and equip if mana allows.
Turn 2: Develop board with Thalia, equip Batterskull, or cast Yorion if ahead.
Prioritize playing lands to ensure consistent casting of 4-drops without stalling.
mid_game: Cast Yorion, Sky Nomad when you control Spirits to trigger massive token generation
Play Solitude immediately after token generation to grant indestructible and +1/+1
Use fetched artifacts to stabilize the board or accelerate token production
Preserve removal for key threats (Tron artifacts, Sneak & Show creatures, Dimir counterspells).
Don't overcommit Yorion unless you can protect it or have a clear path to close.
Use Stoneforge Mystic to find Batterskull early for board control, or save it for Serra Ascendant later.
Manage your own graveyard carefully to avoid feeding Serra Ascendant prematurely or enabling opponent strategies.
Commit threats when you have interaction to protect them or when the board is clear.
Hold back Yorion if facing board wipes or heavy removal.
late_game: Attack with the indestructible Spirit army to close out the game
Maintain mana open to counter removal or board wipes if possible
Leverage Stoneforge Mystic for late-game artifacts that provide value or win conditions
Batterskull-equipped Serra Ascendant, Yorion with flying threats, or a fully equipped Knight of the Reliquary.
Close the game by attacking with Batterskull's lifelink and trample, or flying over blockers with Yorion's +1/+1 counters.
Go all-in when you have card advantage in hand, removal to answer their last interaction, and a clear path to damage.
Commit Yorion when you can protect it or when you're ahead on the board and they lack board wipes.

key_cards.name: Yorion, Sky Nomad
key_cards.role: Primary token generator and combo engine
key_cards.notes: Cast when you control Spirits to maximize the number of 1/1 Spirit tokens created; sequence carefully to avoid leaving yourself vulnerable before Solitude resolves

key_cards.name: Solitude
key_cards.role: Enchantment that buffs and protects Spirits
key_cards.notes: Play immediately after Yorion, Sky Nomad triggers to grant indestructible and +1/+1 to the entire token army, making them immune to most removal and combat damage

key_cards.name: Stoneforge Mystic
key_cards.notes: Use to find key artifacts that synergize with Spirits or provide board control; prioritize fetching artifacts that can be played early to establish tempo

sequencing_tips: Always play lands before casting Stoneforge Mystic to ensure you can pay for fetched artifacts
Cast Yorion, Sky Nomad only when you have enough mana to follow up with Solitude on the same turn or immediately after
Hold up mana for counterspells or protection if you suspect opponent interaction
Prioritize playing Solitude before attacking to ensure tokens survive combat damage and removal

Common mistakes include overcommitting Yorion without protection, failing to play Rest in Peace/Leyline when needed, missequencing Stoneforge Mystic (finding Batterskull too late), and ignoring the graveyard.
Unique interactions: Solitude can target any permanent, making it excellent against artifacts and planeswalkers.
Serra Ascendant requires 10+ cards in graveyard, so manage your own graveyard carefully to avoid feeding it prematurely or enabling opponent strategies.

matchups.advice: Race the opponent before they stabilize with board wipes or counterspells; use Solitude early to protect tokens from removal
Use Solitude to make tokens larger and indestructible for blocking; prioritize early Stoneforge Mystic fetches for defensive artifacts
Disrupt their setup with early interaction; focus on fetching artifacts that provide disruption or race their win condition

Use Solitude to answer their counterspells or key permanents. Play around counters by casting threats at end step or using instant-speed interaction. Don't overcommit Yorion into a locked board; trade efficiently and wait for them to blink or tap out.

Apply early pressure with Thalia and early threats. Use Solitude to remove their ramp sources or finishers. Play Rest in Peace to disrupt their graveyard and force them to interact with your board instead of accelerating.

Win by out-resourcing with Yorion, better sequencing of Stoneforge Mystic, and more efficient removal. Prioritize trading creatures and using Solitude to answer their key threats. Control the board with Thalia and equipment, and force them to make the first mistake.

common_threats: Counterspells targeting Yorion, Sky Nomad or Solitude
Artifact destruction targeting Stoneforge Mystic or fetched artifacts
Watch for counterspells (Dimir), artifact acceleration (Tron), graveyard recursion (Sneak & Show), and early aggressive threats (Delver, Ocelot).
Note when opponents tap out for big spells or hold up interaction.
Track graveyard contents if they play graveyard strategies, and adjust your hate spells accordingly.
